1.title: Provider Config & Secrets
1.instruction: Create provider configs (Google, GitHub) with scopes and redirect URIs. Load client IDs/secrets from environment per env and fail fast when missing.

2.title: State/Nonce Middleware
2.instruction: Implement middleware/helpers to generate, store, and validate state+nonce. Ensure CSRF protection and short-lived storage (cookie or server session).

3.title: Auth Redirect Endpoint
3.instruction: Create the `/auth/:provider/start` endpoint to issue state/nonce, build provider URLs, and redirect the browser. Log start events for audit.

4.title: Callback Handler
4.instruction: Handle `/auth/:provider/callback`, verify state/nonce, and surface provider error codes cleanly. Record metrics for success/failure.

5.title: Token Exchange & Validation
5.instruction: Exchange authorization codes for tokens, validate ID token claims (issuer, audience, expiry), and normalize provider profiles.

6.title: Session Issuance
6.instruction: Issue first-party session (cookie/JWT) with secure attributes, expiry, and refresh/rotation policy. Store minimal identity and revoke on logout.

7.title: User Linking & Account Rules
7.instruction: Implement user lookup/upsert by provider ID/email, handle conflicts, and enforce required profile fields before enabling access.

8.title: Frontend Login UI
8.instruction: Add login buttons, start redirect flow, and render success/error states post-callback. Include loading, retry, and provider error messaging.

9.title: Audit Logging & Tests
9.instruction: Add structured logs for start/callback/token exchange outcomes and negative paths. Cover happy/edge cases with automated tests, including new provider and policy changes.

10.title: Additional Provider (Microsoft)
10.instruction: Add Microsoft as a provider with its scopes and redirect URIs. Reuse the shared flow for start/callback while keeping configs isolated per provider.

11.title: Domain Allowlist & Step-Up MFA
11.instruction: Enforce email domain allowlist post-token validation and require step-up MFA for privileged roles. Deny/redirect unauthorized domains with clear messaging.
